brief_summary: Three-panel glyph strip for the H2 'The rounded bump, geometrically'. Each panel sketches one tactile bump shape on a baseline. Panel 1 'sharp' — narrow spike (Cherry MX Brown). Panel 2 'D-shaped' — steep climb, flat plateau, steep drop (Boba U4T, Tecsee Sapphire). Panel 3 'rounded' — bordeaux-emphasized smooth arc with a coral wash and focal dot, blending into a falling curve that meets the baseline at the bottom-out (Holy Panda lineage / Drop HPX). The visual argument matches the article body: the HPX is geometrically different, not just a spec variant.
data_sources: drop-holy-panda-x-deep-dive.mdx § 'The rounded bump, geometrically' — 'A Cherry MX Brown delivers what bench testers call a sharp bump ... A Boba U4T or a Tecsee Sapphire delivers a D-shaped bump ... The Holy Panda lineage delivers neither. The rounded bump on a Halo-derived stem is a smooth, symmetric arc — gentler entry than the U4T, longer dwell than the Brown, and a fall-off that blends into the bottom-out rather than dropping off cleanly first.'
